extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won the sprint at Qinzhou after BORA-hansgrohe executed textbook leadout, narrowly beating JAKOBSEN Fabio despite torrential conditions.
tactical_patterns: BORA-hansgrohe leadout executed flawlessly in torrential rain, suggesting superior wet-weather train coordination over sprinter teams.
Five-rider break with strong TTers DOWSETT, GRIVKO, FENG kept short leash by Lotto-Jumbo, indicating sprint teams confident controlling weak break.
CAVAGNA Rémi collected bonus seconds at multiple sprints from break, revealing GC ambitions before mountain stages later in week.
GROENEWEGEN Dylan beaten despite holding race lead, suggesting BORA's leadout technical superiority neutralizes UAE's numerical advantage in sprint trains.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: fourth World Tour win of 2018, demonstrates ability to execute in treacherous wet conditions against quality field
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: second place shows competitive sprint speed despite young age and difficult conditions
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: beaten while in race leader jersey suggests vulnerability when teams target him specifically with organized leadouts
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: neutral: aggressive bonus second hunting from break indicates targeting GC on mountain stages, not sprints
rider_signals.DOWSETT Alex: neutral: two consecutive days in break reveals form and willingness to animate races opportunistically
rider_signals.GRIVKO Andrey: neutral: consecutive break attempts show strong current condition but inability to stay away on flat terrain
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: DNS stage 2, career-ending abandon in difficult weather conditions
